name: MultiSelect

unions.name: Msg
unions.comment: Opaque type representing cases to be passed to MultiSelect.update

unions.name: SmartSelect
unions.comment: The opaque type representing a particular smart select instance.

values.name: init
values.comment:
Instantiates and returns a smart select.

  - `selectionMsg` takes a function that expects a tuple representing the list of selections and a MultiSelect.Msg and returns an externally defined msg for handling selection.
  - `internalMsg` takes a function that expects a MultiSelect.Msg and returns an externally defined msg for handling the update of the select.
  - `idPrefix` takes a string with a unique prefix
values.type: { selectionMsg : ( List.List a, MultiSelect.Msg a ) -> msg, internalMsg : MultiSelect.Msg a -> msg, idPrefix : String.String } -> MultiSelect.SmartSelect msg a

values.name: subscriptions
values.comment: Events external to the smart select to which it is subscribed.
values.type: MultiSelect.SmartSelect msg a -> Platform.Sub.Sub msg

values.name: update
values.comment: Update the provided smart select and receive the updated select instance and a cmd to run.
values.type: MultiSelect.Msg a -> MultiSelect.SmartSelect msg a -> ( MultiSelect.SmartSelect msg a, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: updatePosition
values.comment: Triggers an update of the provided smart select's alignment
values.type: MultiSelect.SmartSelect msg a -> ( MultiSelect.SmartSelect msg a, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: view
values.comment:
The smart select view for selecting multiple options at a time with local data.

  - `selected` takes a list of the currently selected entities.
  - `options` takes a list of the data being selected from.
  - `optionLabelFn` takes a function that expects an instance of the data being selected from and returns a string naming/labeling the instance, i.e. if it is a "Product" being selected, the label may be "Garden Hose".
  - `viewSelectedOptionFn` takes a function that expects and instance of the data being selected from and returns html to render a selected option.
  - `settings` takes the settings record (see the `Settings` type)
values.type: { selected : List.List a, options : List.List a, optionLabelFn : a -> String.String, viewSelectedOptionFn : a -> Html.Html msg, settings : SmartSelect.Settings.Settings } -> MultiSelect.SmartSelect msg a -> Html.Html msg

values.name: viewCustom
values.comment: The smart select custom view for selecting multiple options at a time with local data. You have to pass a custom configuration here.
values.type: MultiSelect.Config msg a -> MultiSelect.SmartSelect msg a -> Html.Html msg

name: MultiSelectRemote

unions.name: Msg
unions.comment: Opaque type representing cases to be passed to MultiSelectRemote.update

unions.name: SmartSelect
unions.comment: The opaque type representing a particular smart select instance.

values.name: init
values.comment:
Instantiates and returns a smart select.

  - `selectionMsg` takes a function that expects a tuple representing the list of selections and a MultiSelectRemote.Msg and returns an externally defined msg for handling selection.
  - `internalMsg` takes a function that expects a MultiSelectRemote.Msg and returns an externally defined msg.
  - `idPrefix` takes a string with a unique prefix
values.type: { selectionMsg : ( List.List a, MultiSelectRemote.Msg a ) -> msg, internalMsg : MultiSelectRemote.Msg a -> msg, idPrefix : String.String } -> MultiSelectRemote.SmartSelect msg a

values.name: subscriptions
values.comment: Events external to the smart select to which it is subscribed.
values.type: SmartSelect.Settings.RemoteSettings a -> MultiSelectRemote.SmartSelect msg a -> Platform.Sub.Sub msg

values.name: update
values.comment: Update the provided smart select and receive the updated select instance and a cmd to run.
values.type: MultiSelectRemote.Msg a -> SmartSelect.Settings.RemoteSettings a -> MultiSelectRemote.SmartSelect msg a -> ( MultiSelectRemote.SmartSelect msg a, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: updatePosition
values.comment: Triggers an update of the provided smart select's alignment
values.type: MultiSelectRemote.SmartSelect msg a -> ( MultiSelectRemote.SmartSelect msg a, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: view
values.comment:
The smart select view for selecting multiple options at a time with remote data.

  - `selected` takes a list of the currently selected entities.
  - `optionLabelFn` takes a function that expects an instance of the data being selected from and returns a string naming/labeling the instance, i.e. if it is a "Product" being selected, the label may be "Garden Hose".
  - `viewSelectedOptionFn` takes a function that expects and instance of the data being selected from and returns html to render a selected option.
  - `remoteSettings` takes the remoteSettings record (see the `RemoteSettings` type)
values.type: { selected : List.List a, optionLabelFn : a -> String.String, viewSelectedOptionFn : a -> Html.Html msg, remoteSettings : SmartSelect.Settings.RemoteSettings a } -> MultiSelectRemote.SmartSelect msg a -> Html.Html msg

values.name: viewCustom
values.comment: The smart select custom view for selecting multiple options at a time with remote data. You have to pass a custom configuration here.
values.type: MultiSelectRemote.Config msg a -> MultiSelectRemote.SmartSelect msg a -> Html.Html msg

name: SingleSelect

unions.name: Msg
unions.comment: Opaque type representing cases to be passed to SingleSelect.update

unions.name: SmartSelect
unions.comment: The opaque type representing a particular smart select instance.

values.name: init
values.comment:
Instantiates and returns a smart select.

  - `selectionMsg` takes a function that expects a tuple representing the selection and a SinglSelect.Msg msg and returns an externally defined msg for handling selection.
  - `internalMsg` takes a function that expects a SinglSelect.Msg and returns an externally defined msg for handling the update of the select.
  - `idPrefix` takes a string with a unique prefix
values.type: { selectionMsg : ( a, SingleSelect.Msg a ) -> msg, internalMsg : SingleSelect.Msg a -> msg, idPrefix : String.String } -> SingleSelect.SmartSelect msg a

values.name: subscriptions
values.comment: Events external to the smart select to which it is subscribed.
values.type: SingleSelect.SmartSelect msg a -> Platform.Sub.Sub msg

values.name: update
values.comment: Update the provided smart select and receive the updated select instance and a cmd to run.
values.type: SingleSelect.Msg a -> SingleSelect.SmartSelect msg a -> ( SingleSelect.SmartSelect msg a, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: updatePosition
values.comment: Triggers an update of the provided smart select's alignment
values.type: SingleSelect.SmartSelect msg a -> ( SingleSelect.SmartSelect msg a, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: view
values.comment:
The smart select view for selecting one option at a time with local data.

  - `selected` takes the currently selected entity, if any.
  - `options` takes a list of the data being selected from.
  - `optionLabelFn` takes a function that expects an instance of the data being selected from and returns a string naming/labeling the instance, i.e. if it is a "Product" being selected, the label may be "Garden Hose".
  - `settings` takes the settings record (see the `Settings` type)
values.type: { selected : Maybe.Maybe a, options : List.List a, optionLabelFn : a -> String.String, settings : SmartSelect.Settings.Settings } -> SingleSelect.SmartSelect msg a -> Html.Html msg

values.name: viewCustom
values.comment: The smart select view for selecting one option at a time with local data. You have to pass a custom configuration here.
values.type: SingleSelect.Config a -> SingleSelect.SmartSelect msg a -> Html.Html msg

name: SmartSelect.Settings

aliases.name: RemoteQueryAttrs
aliases.comment: Fields to be provided to facilitate the external request. The function provided to url takes in searchText in the event it is necessary for the query.
aliases.type: { headers : List.List Http.Header, url : String.String -> String.String, optionDecoder : Json.Decode.Decoder a }

aliases.name: RemoteSettings
aliases.comment: The type representing the select's remote settings (including the general settings) to be passed to all view & update functions of the remote variants
aliases.type: { settings : SmartSelect.Settings.Settings, spinnerColor : Color.Color, characterThresholdPrompt : Basics.Int -> String.String, characterSearchThreshold : Basics.Int, debounceDuration : Basics.Float, queryErrorMsg : String.String, queryAttrs : SmartSelect.Settings.RemoteQueryAttrs a }

aliases.name: Settings
aliases.comment: The type representing the select's general settings to be passed to all view functions
aliases.type: { theme : SmartSelect.Settings.Theme, isDisabled : Basics.Bool, optionsContainerMaxHeight : Basics.Float, placeholder : String.String, noResultsForMsg : String.String -> String.String, noOptionsMsg : String.String, id : String.String }

aliases.name: Theme
aliases.comment: The type facilitating the Theme with the most important design tokens
aliases.type: { fontSize : { base : Css.Px, sm : Css.Px, xs : Css.Px, xxs : Css.Px }, color : { text : { primary : Css.Color, secondary : Css.Color, disabled : Css.Color }, primary : { main : Css.Color, contrastText : Css.Color, light : Css.Color }, error : { main : Css.Color, contrastText : Css.Color, light : Css.Color }, background : { input : Css.Color, optionsContainer : Css.Color }, action : { hover : Css.Color }, border : Css.Color }, size : { iconButton : Css.Px, inputElement : Css.Px }, borderWidth : Css.Px, borderRadius : { base : Css.Px, lg : Css.Px }, boxShadow : { offsetX : Css.Px, offsetY : Css.Px, blurRadius : Css.Px, spreadRadius : Css.Px, color : Css.Color }, transition : { duration : Basics.Float }, classNamePrefix : String.String }

values.name: defaultRemoteSettings
values.comment:
A record of default settings for the remote select variants. Extend this if
you want to further customize.

Requires the `RemoteQueryAttrs`.
values.type: SmartSelect.Settings.RemoteQueryAttrs a -> SmartSelect.Settings.RemoteSettings a

values.name: defaultSettings
values.comment:
A record of default settings for all the select variants. Extend this if
you want to further customize.
values.type: SmartSelect.Settings.Settings

values.name: defaultTheme
values.comment: The default theme that is included in the defaultSettings
values.type: SmartSelect.Settings.Theme
